L1.area: Database driver schema introspection and metadata retrieval layer

L2.description: A SQL injection vulnerability exists where user-controlled table name arguments are directly interpolated into SQL query strings without sanitization or parameterization. An attacker who can influence the table name passed to schema introspection methods can inject arbitrary SQL statements, potentially leading to unauthorized data access or modification.

L3.area: Database driver schema introspection and metadata retrieval layer
L3.description: A SQL injection vulnerability exists where user-controlled table name arguments are directly interpolated into SQL query strings without sanitization or parameterization. An attacker who can influence the table name passed to schema introspection methods can inject arbitrary SQL statements, potentially leading to unauthorized data access or modification.

ground_truth.reason: The sqlite3 driver directly interpolates user-controlled table name parameters into SQL queries in its schema introspection methods, enabling SQL injection when these methods receive untrusted input.
